mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.94% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained FEDFUNDS above 4.5% signals ongoing tightening that increases equity discount rates and reduces forward earnings valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS at 135,106k) combined with unemployment rate stability at 4.6% indicates a tight labor market approaching full employment. This late-cycle labor tightness signals wage pressure and margin compression, producing negative equity returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.8% while PAYEMS growth exceeds 100k/month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% and PAYEMS at 135,106k as of May 2006
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE shows Rank IC < 0.05 when tested as an interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 364,198M) show sustained consumer spending momentum. A month-over-month acceleration in RSAFS above 0.5% signals robust demand that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following RSAFS acceleration > 0.5% are negative in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 112.0991) reflects real economic capacity utilization. A decline in INDPRO month-over-month signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO declines > 0.3% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 1,849k) and real GDP growth (GDPC1 at 11,394.7B) jointly determine construction-led demand momentum. When both HOUST and GDPC1 show quarter-over-quarter growth above trend, equity valuations expand due to multiplier effects, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: construction-led expansion with strong GDP growth
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect of HOUST and GDPC1 shows Rank IC < 0.05 when tested jointly.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL (201.9) combined with elevated FEDFUNDS (4.94%) indicates the Fed is in active tightening mode to combat price pressures. When CPI year-over-year growth exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, real rates are restrictive and equity valuations compress, producing negative returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both CPI YoY > 3% and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 for the interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE at 4.6%) at cyclical lows signals labor market tightness. A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months indicates labor market deterioration and signals recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration signaling late-cycle weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure productive capacity and labor utilization. When both INDPRO and PAYEMS show sustained month-over-month growth above 0.2%, the economy is operating at high capacity with strong productivity, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.2% and PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1%
regime_hint: strong supply-side expansion with robust employment growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect of INDPRO and PAYEMS shows Rank IC < 0.05 when tested jointly over any 2-year rolling window.
